title: Kanakadhara Stotram
intro: Read Kanakadhara Stotram with Hindi meaning. Chant to receive the divine blessings of Goddess Lakshmi.
description:
### Shri Kanakadhara Stotram

1. Angahare pulakabhushana mashrayanti bhringanganaiva mukulabharanam tamalam.
Angikritakhila vibhutirapangalila mangalyadastu mama mangaladevata yah.

2. Mugdhya muhurvidadhati vadanai muraraih prematrapapranihitani gatagatani.
Mala drishormadhukara vimahotpale ya sa mai shriyam dishatu sagara sambhavayah.

3. Vishvamarendra padavibhramadanadaksha mananda hetu radhikam madhuvidvishopi.
Ishannishidatu mayi kshanamikshanardham indovarodara sahodara mindirayah.

4. Amilitakshamadhigamya muda mukundam anandakanda manimeshamanangatantram.
Akekara sthita kani nikapakshma netram bhutyai bhavennama bhujangarayang anayah.

5. Bahyantare madhujitah shritakaustubhai ya haravaliva harinimilamayi vibhati.
Kamaprada bhagavato pi katakshamala kalyana bhavahatu me kamalalayah.

6. Kalambudalalilolarsi kaithabharer dharadhare sphurati ya tadidanganai va.
Matuh samasta jagatam mahaniya murtibhadramni me dishatu bhargavanandanayah.

7. Praptam padam prathamatah kila yatprabhavan mangalya bhajih madhumayani manmathena.
Madhyapateta diha manthara mikshanardham mandalasam cha makaralaya kanakayah.

8. Dadyad dayanu pavano dravinambudhara masmibhakinchan vihanga shishau vishanna.
Dushkarmadharmam apaniya chiraya duram narayana pranayini nayanambuvah.

9. Ishta vishishtamatayo pi yatha yayardradrishtya trivishtapapadam sulabham labhante.
Drishtih prahrushta kamalodara dipti rishtam pushti krishishta mama pushkara vishtarayah.

10. Girdavaiti garudadhvaja bhaminiti shakambhariti shashishekhara vallabheti.
Srishti sthiti pralaya kelishu samsthitayai tasyai namastri bhuvanai kagurostarunyai.

11. Shrutyai namostu shubhakarmaphala prasutyai ratyai namostu ramaniya gunarnavaiyai.
Shaktyai namostu shatapatra niketanayai pushtyai namostu purushottama vallabhayai.

12. Namostu nalika nibhananayai namostu dugdhaudadhi janma bhutyai.
Namostu somamrita sodarayai namostu narayana vallabhayai.

13. Sampatkara ni sakalendriya nandani samrajyadana vibhavani saroru hakshi.
Tva dvandanani durita haranadyatani mamevamatar nisham kalayantu nanyam.

14. Yatkataksha samupasana vidhih sevakasya kalartha sampadah.
Santanoti vachanangamanasamsatvam murarihridayeshvarim bhaje.

15. Sarasijanilaye sarojahaste dhavalamashukagandhamalya shobhe.
Bhagavati harivallabhe manojne tribhuvanabhutikari prasida mahyam.

16. Dagdhistimih kanakumbhamukha va srishtisvarvahini vimalacharu jala plutangim.
Pratarnamami jagatam jananimashesha lokadhinatha grihini mamritab dhiputrim.

17. Kamale kamalakshavallabhe tvam karunapurataram gatairapardangaih.
Avalokaya ma kinchananam prathamam patramakritrimam dayayah.

18. Stuvanti ye stutibhir bhumiranvaham trayimayim tribhuvanamataram ramam.
Gunadhika gurutara bhagyabhagino bhavanti te budhabhavitayah.

॥ Iti Shri Kanakadhara Stotram Sampurnam ॥

faq.q1: Who composed the Shri Kanakadhara Stotram?
faq.a1: The Shri Kanakadhara Stotram was composed by Adi Shankaracharya.
faq.q2: What are the benefits of reciting the Kanakadhara Stotram?
faq.a2: It is believed that the regular recitation of this stotram helps in attracting prosperity and wealth, removes financial distress (poverty), and invokes the special blessings of Goddess Lakshmi.
faq.q3: What is the most auspicious time to recite the Kanakadhara Stotram?
faq.a3: While it can be recited on any day, Friday is considered particularly auspicious as it is dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi. Reciting it early in the morning after a bath is considered the most effective.
faq.q4: What is the legend behind this stotram?
faq.a4: When Adi Shankaracharya went to a very poor woman's house to beg for alms, she had nothing to offer but a single dry amla (gooseberry). Moved by her devotion, Shankaracharya immediately chanted the 'Kanakadhara Stotram.' Pleased by his prayer, Goddess Lakshmi showered the woman's home with golden amlas.
faq.q5: Are there any special precautions to take while reciting the Kanakadhara Stotram?
faq.a5: One should recite it with complete faith and a pure heart. If possible, it is recommended to recite it in front of an idol or picture of Goddess Lakshmi, preferably by lighting a ghee lamp and wearing clean clothes.
